title: The Peninsula Golf Weekend: Two Nights, One Serious Round, Room for the Rest
dek: One public-access course with genuine ranking credibility, a long lunch on the ridge, a hot springs session, and a stay within ten minutes of the first tee. For groups where not everyone plays.
editorNote: The Peninsula golf weekend that actually makes sense for a group where not everyone plays. One serious round at St Andrews Beach — Tom Doak architecture, public access, world top-100 credibility — anchors Saturday. The rest of the weekend shapes around it: ridge stays, vineyard lunches, coastal walks for the non-golfers, and a Sunday that sends you home slowly through Red Hill. Not a 36-hole grind. A shaped weekend that uses golf as the hero round rather than the only event. Book St Andrews Beach 4+ weeks out; plan the lunch at Montalto the same day.

stops.note: Arrive Friday afternoon, check in, settle. The ridge stay positions both golf days and the non-golf days properly — Cape Schanck is 15 minutes south, Red Hill restaurants are at your door.
Friday dinner at the hotel restaurant. Sets the weekend up as an event rather than a golf grind.

Saturday morning tee time at St Andrews Beach. Book 4+ weeks ahead. Links country, firm fairways, Tom Doak greens. Allow 4.5 hours for 18 with walk-ups. Non-golfers drive to Cape Schanck for the boardwalk and Bushrangers Bay.
Late lunch at Montalto piazza — relaxed, no dress code required, close enough to the course to work as a recovery meal. The whole group meets here whether they played or not.
Late afternoon walk for the non-golfers (or an easy option for everyone). Southern Ocean cliffs and the light at 5pm. Optional — skip and rest if the morning round was tiring.
Sunday brunch in Mornington. Strong coffee, good eggs, no queue before 9am. Shake out the Saturday legs.
One cellar door before the drive home. Serious pinot noir and chardonnay. The ridge sending you off.

heroImage.alt: Coastal golf course landscape on the Mornington Peninsula
